reviews.review_description: Very nice application, impressive will to keep it simple. Just missing a important feature: Export

This is one of those apps that Meta crippled with their boundary system. No, I don't want to map the whole house and never move anything before using an app like SeeSignal. Disabling the boundary system does work, but it requires developer mode and it will deactivate passthrough for many apps ... because, when you don't have a boundary to protect you, it's safest to disable your eyes too! /s
This app is great, and even more important, it’s likely to inspire more types of spatial computing in the future. Love it! One of the first things I show most new users of VR
Finally was able to see not just signal strength in the corners of my home, but outside as well. Thank you for this great app.

By desactivating guardian on quest 2, I was able to use this app while walking everywhere inside my home and in the garage.  I could also head outside and determine the limits where my wifi will have a bad signal.

I can see that this app could be a valuable tool for enterprise looking to determine wifi issues.

Now, if only there was a way to insert a sim card inside the quest headset to test out LTE or 5G signal!

So it's a neat idea each node shows signal strength, but it doesn't show what Wi-Fi hotspot is connected to, so if you're in a small apartment then it's hard to tell where the interfering Wi-Fi signal is coming from.

reviews.review_title: Excuse me does it work on quest1?
reviews.review_description: I could not find any signal even if I was close to my router
I will write a review on this later this weekend!

This app showed me exactly where my strongest Wi-Fi signal was and as a result, the apps I used were smoother than normal.  The tutorial made it easy to get started. 

Now when I take my Quest 2 to others homes, I'll know exactly where to set it up based on the strongest internet connection. Wow, this app is so useful.

The idea of showing wifi strength like this isn’t new (there’s at least one AR phone app from a few years ago), but in MR it potentially can be more practical to work with, allowing handsfree usage.

Interacting with the signal bars is easy, but I don’t understand why you’re showing the ones for which there is no signal measurement yet. It suggests some signal detection/prediction is being done at those locations, but that clearly isn’t the case.

The HUD is a nice idea. The arrow didn’t see to point to the right direction though (ie roughly at my wifi AP). 

The biggest problem, unrelated to the app itself, is the Quest OS limit of having to set up a guardian with a fixed size. That simply doesn’t work when roaming around the house, and having the signal bars disappear when going outside the guardian. When the Quest OS becomes a real mixed reality OS (as HoloLens provides) such things will hopefully be solved.

I have used SeeSignal to troubleshoot connectivity issues in my place many times. 
SeeSignal is a must- have for anyone working or gaming from home! 
I cannot wait to see what is next on this app!
Extremely well written and implemented and it works! Walk around and see your wifi signal. See where it's strongest and weakest. So many applications for this technology.
